title: Literacy Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Literacy Programs 19 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific services and information to promote optimal health. On site food and child care are available for scheduled events. Direct services include: -Parenting support and educational services aimed at mobilizing Black parents to ensure their children achieve educational excellence -Employment services: development, placement, and support -Family Literacy and STEAM fairs -Cultivating Culture: Gardening and healthy eating program -Advocacy for African American families with young children in need of early intervention/early childhood special education -Parent University classes and experiences focused on: Parental engagement in children's education, effective parenting, and all areas of optimal health (intellectual, physical, emotional, spiritual, socio-economic, and relationships) 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Classes are usually held Thursday nights. Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF SOUTHWESTERN OREGON BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 3333 Walnut Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: -Varies by program -Teen Center hours: Monday-Thursday 2:45pm-6:30pm, Friday 12:15pm-6:30pm -Summer extended hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (541) 267-3635 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool with list of Family Resource Centers (FRCs) by school district. Provides a range of services and support to families with children, including child literacy groups and story times, playgroups, assistance with accessing school personnel, parent education, parent literacy and computer support, and other drop-in services for families and children. Some locations may offer children's clothing and other supplies. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one tutoring. Students learn to read, write, learn English as a second language, improve basic math skills, and prepare for the citizenship test. Assists to improve job skills, pass the written driver's exam, fill out job applications and other forms, read medicine labels and signs, and learn to use a computer in an educational capacity. Offers a specialized program designed to help those with the learning disability dyslexia learn to read, write, and spell. Also helps students qualify for enrollment at Rogue Community College (RCC) and assists them in preparing for their GED in language arts, math, science, and social studies. 132 NE B Street Suite 23 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 472-0355 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-VALLEY LITERACY CENTER ADULT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides literacy services. Offers computer basics, adult English classes for English Language Learners, workplace English and Spanish Language Literacy, preparation for GED, United States citizenship, and Certified Nursing Assistants certifications. 1850 45th Avenue NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-8pm Email (503) 463-1488 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY ADULT LITERACY TUTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one tutors to assist adults with GED preparation, English literacy (reading, writing, and speaking), and life skills (reading, writing, and math for daily life). Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Tutoring hours vary Email (503) 577-9984 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details SMART READING SMART READING CENTRAL AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits volunteers to read with children, fostering a love of reading and providing books for children to keep and add to their personal libraries. The North Coast Team is based out of SMART's main office in Portland, but staff regularly visis the areas covered by this team. 1029 NW 14th St Suite 101 Suite C Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Literacy program participants: Ages 5-10 (Pre-K through grade 3) Volunteers: Must undergo a national criminal background check Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 797-7726 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SMART READING SMART READING DOUGLAS AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits volunteers to read with children, fostering a love of reading and providing books for children to keep and add to their personal libraries. The North Coast Team is based out of SMART's main office in Portland, but staff regularly visis the areas covered by this team. The North Coast Team is based out of SMART's main office in Portland, but staff regularly visis the areas covered by this team. 500 Main Street Suite B Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Literacy program participants: Ages 5-10 (Pre-K through grade 3) Volunteers: Must undergo a national criminal background check Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 600-8035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SILVERTON SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Encourages the Spanish-speaking community to share their culture and diverse backgrounds. Works with Latino parents and students to provide the knowledge and resources needed to graduate from high school and enter college. 512 N 1st Street Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Nuestros Abuelos: Older adults who identify as Hispanic or Latino Juntos: Families who identify as Hispanic or Latino and have a student in grades 8-12 Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 873-7114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tutoring and adult literacy program that offers one-on-one and small group instruction in reading, writing, GED preparation, and English as a Second Language (ESL) courses. 736 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and adults who identify as having low literacy skills Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 672-6336 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YACHATS YOUTH AND FAMILY ACTIVITIES PROGRAM YACHATS COMMUNITY PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH FAMILIES TOGETHER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers dinner and a book reading once a month. A copy of the featured story for the night goes home with every family. There is always a story-themed craft for the children to do after dinner. Free chapter books are available for older siblings who attend. 360 W 7th Street Yachats, OR 97498 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 4th Friday of the month 6pm-8pm (September-May) Email (541) 547-3400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF SOUTHWESTERN OREGON BOYS & GIRLS CLUB EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION MID-VALLEY LITERACY CENTER ADULT LITERACY PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD YOUTH & FAMILIES SMART READING SMART READING CENTRAL AREA SMART READING SMART READING DOUGLAS AREA SMART READING SMART READING METRO AREA SMART READING SMART READING NORTHWEST AREA SMART READING SMART READING ROGUE VALLEY AREA SMART READING SMART READING SOUTH CENTRAL AREA SMART READING SMART READING SOUTH COAST AREA SMART READING SMART READING SOUTH VALLEY AREA SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SILVERTON SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY YACHATS YOUTH AND FAMILY ACTIVITIES PROGRAM FAMILIES TOGETHER
